title: Causal tyre-cliff detector instead of static degradation totals
summary: F1’s tyre-performance graphic and recent state-space work both frame tyre pace as a latent process that evolves through the stint and resets at pit stops.
rationale: F1’s tyre-performance graphic and recent state-space work both frame tyre pace as a latent process that evolves through the stint and resets at pit stops. The state-space paper reports better predictive behavior than a simpler AR(1) benchmark, suggesting that raw cumulative degradation is too blunt to detect the last few laps before a stop.
implementation_hint: Within each `driver-race-stint`, sort causally and compute rolling slope, acceleration, EWMA residuals, and short-horizon change scores for `LapTime_Delta`, `LapTime (s)`, and `Cumulative_Degradation`. Add warm-up-reset features after a pit and a simple one-step latent-pace proxy that restarts on each new stint.
expected_effect: Should sharpen discrimination on ambiguous pre-stop laps, especially where degradation turns nonlinear late in a stint.
risk: Short stints are noisy, and any use of pit-entry/out-laps or across-stint smoothing will leak reset information.
